evidence_strength: committed source plus five passing isolated Node tests

primary_sources.title: Performance Timeline
primary_sources.use: PerformanceEntry startTime and duration provide a shared high-resolution performance timeline.

primary_sources.title: High Resolution Time Level 3
primary_sources.use: DOMHighResTimeStamp supplies the monotonic time basis used by performance entries.

primary_sources.title: Long Animation Frames API
primary_sources.use: Long-animation-frame entries expose main-thread congestion windows.

primary_sources.title: Event Timing API
primary_sources.use: Event Timing entries expose interaction windows and interactionId grouping.

finding: Event Timing and Long Animation Frame entries can be correlated by interval overlap because both are PerformanceEntry records on the same performance timeline. Overlap is temporal co-occurrence, not causal proof.

covered: privacy exclusion for DOM targets and script URLs

privacy_boundary: The artifact exports interaction IDs, event type, aggregate durations, and anonymous frame indexes only. DOM targets, selectors, script URLs, raw user agents, and private content are excluded.
claim_limit: A shared-timeline overlap establishes temporal co-occurrence only. It does not establish that frame congestion caused interaction latency, identify responsible source code, or prove subjective harm.
falsification_route: Collect browser PerformanceObserver entries for event and long-animation-frame, compare harness overlap intervals against a DevTools trace, and reject the method if entry timestamps use incompatible origins, interval arithmetic disagrees materially with trace windows, or buffered-entry loss makes classifications unstable.
